other_observers: none; Kara Lewantowicz reported to me, that she and Bill Maynard saw a late Baird's Sandpiper at Pueblo Reservoir at the Sailboard launching area on November 26. 2005. This is likely the same bird.
specific_location: Along the Arkansas River, below Pueblo Reservoir dam, in the Rock Canyon/Valco Ponds area.
description: A shorebird with black legs and long wings, that was not that was about an inch or two bigger than the nearby Least Sandpiper. The bird had a black bill and buffy front and white underneath. See photos.Behaviors: On the rocks along the Arkansas River with a Least Sandpiper and Killdeer.Call: nonePlumage: adult in winter plumage
